beta.warning: You are using the beta version. Features may be unstable - keep a backup of your save files.

home.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Save Editor
home.description: Drop your Player.sav, Mii.sav, or Map.sav to start editing. Each file opens in the matching tab.

error.not_found_description: We couldn't find the page you were looking for. It may have moved, or the link may be wrong.
error.generic_description: An unexpected error occurred while loading this page.

history.title: Drop history
history.description: A local copy of every save file you've dropped into the editor, kept in your browser only. Use it as a last-chance recovery if you accidentally save over your real backup.
history.warning_title: This is not a backup solution.
history.warning_body: Files here live only in this browser and can be wiped at any time by clearing site data, switching browsers, private windows, or storage pressure. Always keep a real backup of your Mii.sav, Player.sav, and Map.sav files somewhere safe.
history.empty: No drops recorded yet. The next save file you load will appear here.
history.delete_body: Remove this saved drop from this browser. The original .sav files in the snapshot will be lost from history.
history.delete_warning: This can't be undone. If this is your only copy of these files, download the zip first.
history.clear_body: Remove every saved drop from this browser. Any .sav files only kept here will be gone.

toast.quota_exceeded: Drop history is full and your browser is out of storage. Open History and delete older drops to make room.
toast.save_failed: Could not save this drop to history.
toast.download_failed: Could not read the saved files for this drop.

ugc_editor.title: UGC editor
ugc_editor.description: Replace UGC textures (clothes, food, goods, exteriors, interiors, map objects, map floors) with your own PNG images. Loads .canvas.zs and .ugctex.zs files from your save folder.
ugc_editor.needs_player: Load {playerSav} from the Player tab to begin.
ugc_editor.needs_player_or_folder: Load {playerSav} from the Player tab, or pick a Ugc/ folder below to start editing textures without it.
ugc_editor.sidecar_only_notice: Editing without {playerSav}: some features may not be available.

dropzone.title: Drop your Ugc/ folder or a zip here
dropzone.hint: All .canvas.zs / .ugctex.zs files inside will be loaded.

sidecar.hint: Drop your save folder or a zip of the Ugc/ directory. Shared with the ShareMii tab.

list.title: Items ({count})
list.empty: No items found in this category.

editor.pick_item: Select an item from the list to edit.
editor.drop_png: Click or drop a PNG, JPG or WEBP here
editor.regenerate_thumb_hint: Overwrite the existing thumbnail with one generated from your PNG. Leave unchecked to keep the current thumbnail.

fit_mode.label: Fit
fit_mode.cover_hint: Crop to fill the texture, preserving aspect ratio.
fit_mode.contain_hint: Fit the whole image inside, leaving transparent edges.
fit_mode.fill_hint: Stretch to fill the texture, ignoring aspect ratio.

encoder.label: Encoder
encoder.custom_hint: Tuned for shadows and gradients via gamma-aware fitting. Default.
encoder.rgbcx_hint: Richard Geldreich's reference BC1 encoder. Higher overall quality, weaker on shadow detail.

bc1_mode.label: BC1 mode
bc1_mode.auto_hint: Picks the best mode for each block. Recommended.
bc1_mode.fourColor_hint: Sharper colors. Transparent pixels are still preserved.
bc1_mode.threeColor_hint: Optimized for images with transparency. Less color detail.

lan_restriction.label: LAN restriction
lan_restriction.hint: This item was received over local wireless and is locked from being shared again. Clear it to lift the restriction.

toast.unsupported_image: Drop a PNG, JPG or WEBP file.
toast.no_texture: No texture file for this item yet.
toast.replaced: Replaced slot {slot}. Files staged for download.
toast.reverted: Reverted slot {slot} to its loaded state.

save.drop_warning_text: editing save files can corrupt your game. Always keep a backup of the original file before uploading.

bulk.drop_hint: Drop one or more .sav files, a folder, or a .zip. Each file is routed to the right tab.
bulk.loaded_count: Loaded {count, plural, one {# save} other {# saves}}
bulk.skipped_count: {count, plural, one {# file skipped} other {# files skipped}} (unrecognised).
bulk.none_recognized: No recognised save files found. Expected Player.sav, Mii.sav, or Map.sav.
bulk.overwrite_intro: The following tabs already have a save loaded. Continuing will replace them with the new files:
bulk.overwrite_warning: any unsaved edits in those tabs will be lost. Make sure you've downloaded any changes first.
bulk.clear_intro: This will remove the following saves from the editor:
bulk.clear_warning: any unsaved edits will be lost. Make sure you've downloaded any changes first.

session.restore_intro: Previous session. Restore your last edits, or dismiss to start fresh.
session.restore_note: Saves stay in this browser and are never uploaded. Dismissing here, or using "Clear all", removes them.

player.title: Player
player.description: Core player stats and game progress.
player.empty_state: None of the curated Player fields were found in this save. Use the Advanced section below to browse everything.

ugc_text.heading: UGC text
ugc_text.description: Words and phrases stored in UGC.Text.TextData.*. Used by Mii dialogue, news, and other content that references custom text.
ugc_text.missing: This save doesn't expose the UGC.Text.TextData.* arrays.
ugc_text.empty_hint: No UGC text yet. Use "Add slot" to create one.
ugc_text.how_hint: Phonetic spelling used by voice playback. Leave blank to mirror the text.
ugc_text.clear_confirm: Clear this UGC text slot? Genre, language, text and pronunciation will be reset.
ugc_text.error_split_surrogate: Last character splits an emoji. Drop one.

inventory.bulk_warning: This will overwrite the state and/or quantity of every entry currently visible ({count}). Edits cannot be undone except by re-opening your save. Make sure you have a backup.
inventory.bulk_confirm: Apply this bulk edit to {count} entries? This cannot be undone.

foods.heading: Foods
foods.caption: {count, plural, one {# food} other {# foods}} shown. Mark as Obtained to unlock in your store; OwnNum is the inventory count.
foods.missing: This save does not expose Player.FoodInfo.State or OwnNum, so foods can't be edited here.

clothes.heading: Clothes
clothes.caption: {count, plural, one {# garment} other {# garments}} shown. Mark as Obtained to unlock in your store; OwnNum is the inventory count.
clothes.color_note: Cloth-level controls apply to every color variant. Expand a row to edit a single color.
clothes.missing: This save does not expose Player.ClothInfo.OwnInfoArray.State or OwnNum, so clothes can't be edited here.

clothing_sets.heading: Clothing sets
clothing_sets.caption: {count, plural, one {# clothing set} other {# clothing sets}} shown. Mark as Obtained to unlock the set in your store; OwnNum is the inventory count. A clothing set is a complete outfit a Mii can wear in one slot.
clothing_sets.color_note: Set-level controls apply to every color variant. Expand a row to edit a single color.
clothing_sets.missing: This save does not expose Player.CoordinateInfo.OwnInfoArray.State or OwnNum, so clothing sets can't be edited here.

treasures.heading: Treasures
treasures.caption: {count, plural, one {# treasure} other {# treasures}} shown. Mark as Obtained to add it to your collection; OwnNum is the inventory count.
treasures.missing: This save does not expose any Player.GoodsInfo2 entries, so treasures can't be edited here.

interiors.heading: Interiors
interiors.caption: {count, plural, one {# style group} other {# style groups}} shown. Mark as Obtained to unlock the interior set; OwnNum is the inventory count.
interiors.missing: This save does not expose any Player.InteriorRoomStyleInfo entries, so interiors can't be edited here.
interiors.variant_note: Group-level controls apply to every variant in the set. Expand a row to edit a single variant.

buildings.heading: Buildings
buildings.caption: {count, plural, one {# building} other {# buildings}} shown. Mark as Obtained to unlock in your shop; OwnNum is how many you have placed.
buildings.missing: This save does not expose Player.BuildingInfo2 entries, so buildings can't be edited here.
buildings.variant_note: Item-level controls apply to every color variant. Expand a row to edit a single color.

wishes.heading: Fountain wishes
wishes.caption: {liberated} of {total} wishes unlocked.
wishes.missing: This save does not expose Liberation.WishInfo arrays, so wishes can't be edited here.
wishes.bulk_confirm: Apply this change to {count} wishes? This cannot be undone except by re-opening your save.

gate.count_tip: Unlocks after {count} prior wishes have come true

bump.title: Bump stats to satisfy unlock?
bump.level: One of these wishes requires Fountain Lv. {level}, but yours is lower. Bump your fountain level to {level} so the unlock sticks?
bump.count: One of these wishes unlocks after {count} prior wishes-come-true. Bump your counter to {count} so the unlock sticks?
bump.both: These wishes need Fountain Lv. {level} and {count} prior wishes-come-true. Bump your fountain level and counter to satisfy them?

mii.title: Mii
mii.description: Edit individual Miis on your island.

housing.title: Housing
housing.intro: Move islanders between houses on the map.
housing.map_not_loaded: Load Map.sav to view houses on the island.
housing.no_houses: No houses found on the loaded map.
housing.assign_hint: Pick a house and room for this islander

export.description: Export every Mii and their relationships as a file you can open elsewhere. Your save isn't modified.

belongings.missing: This save does not expose Mii.Belongings.ClothOwnInfo, so belongings can't be edited here.
belongings.worn_caption: What this Mii is actually wearing right now. The game prefers a clothing set when one is selected; the individual slots below are only used when the clothing set is (none). Setting any slot to (none) lets the game pick a default.
belongings.worn_coordinate_caption: A clothing set is a complete outfit. While set, it overrides the individual cloth slots.
belongings.worn_switch_individual_tip: Clear the clothing set and dress this Mii using individual cloth slots
belongings.worn_switch_coordinate_tip: Clear the individual slots and pick a clothing set instead
belongings.worn_individual_blocked_warning: A clothing set is currently active and overrides these slots. Clear it for individual pieces to take effect.
belongings.coords_caption: Clothing sets this Mii personally owns. Toggle individual colors to give or take a set.
belongings.worn_clear_tip: Set this slot to (none) and reset its color to 0
belongings.heading: Wardrobe
belongings.caption: Clothes this Mii personally owns. Toggle individual colors to give or take items. Removing every color of an item the Mii is currently wearing may cause the game to swap them into a default outfit on next load.
belongings.give_all_colors_tip: Give every color of this item
belongings.clear_all_colors_tip: Remove every color of this item
belongings.goods_caption: Goods this Mii is currently carrying around. Each Mii has a fixed set of pocket slots; pick a treasure or gear from the catalog to fill one, or clear it to make the slot empty.
belongings.goods_ugc_caption: Custom Mii-made good. Edit it from the UGC tab; clear this slot to remove it.

habits.missing: This save does not expose Mii.Belongings.HabitOwnInfo.* fields, so habits can't be edited here.
habits.heading: Habits
habits.caption: Personal quirks this Mii has picked up. The radio selects the active (visible in-game) habit per category. The checkbox marks habits the Mii owns but isn't currently displaying.
habits.is_own_tip: Mii has acquired this habit, even if it isn't the one currently active. The active habit is automatically owned.

troubles.complexity_warning_body: There are 115 trouble types, each with up to 27 parameters and many runtime conditions checked by the game itself (story progress, relationships, items in stock, time of day, and more). The editor lets you set any combination, but the game still decides whether the trouble actually fires. Some combinations may silently do nothing or behave unexpectedly.
troubles.complexity_warning_no_support: No support is provided for this tab. If you find a combination that is clearly broken (writes invalid data, crashes the game, corrupts the save), please
troubles.missing: This save does not expose Mii.Trouble.* fields, so troubles can't be edited here.
troubles.section_active_caption: Each Mii can have at most one active trouble. Picking "No trouble" clears any associated targets and timers.
troubles.schedule_caption: Times use your local wall clock (the game stores them as Unix epoch seconds). Empty means disabled.
troubles.reset_schedule_tip: Set Next check = now, Auto-fail = now + {minutes} min (the trouble's default duration).
troubles.reset_trouble_tip: Clear the active trouble for this Mii: zeroes the trouble Id, all targets, and the schedule.
troubles.revert_tip: Restore every trouble field for this Mii to the values from the originally loaded save.
troubles.next_hint: When the engine will next evaluate this Mii's trouble logic. While the slot has no active trouble this acts as the cooldown: the next attempt to roll a new trouble can't fire before this time.
troubles.end_hint: When the active trouble expires. If this is in the past the engine will resolve the trouble as a failure on the next tick.

meta.method_tip: MethodKey: internal handler used by the engine
meta.target_mii_num: {n, plural, one {# target Mii} other {# target Miis}}
meta.target_mii_num_tip: How many Mii indices the engine reads from the save
meta.cooldown_tip: Post-resolution cooldown (NextTimeMinute) before this trouble can re-trigger
meta.rescue_day_tip: Days after which the engine forcibly resolves the trouble in your favor
meta.gated_tip: This trouble is gated by an introduction / tutorial milestone
meta.scripted_tip: GenerateRate = 0: the engine never rolls this trouble at random; it appears only via specific scripted events

troubles.end_before_next_warning: Auto-fail time is before the next check, so the trouble will resolve as a failure on the next tick.
troubles.target_coordinate_hint: Numeric hash from RSDB/Coordinate; leave 0 for none.

panel.no_name_spine: This save doesn't expose the Mii.Name.Name spine - there's nothing to enumerate. Use the Player tab's advanced panel to inspect raw entries.
panel.no_slots: No populated Mii slots were found. Slots are detected by checking Mii.Name.Name for a non-empty string.
panel.fix_zero_charinfoex: Your save appears to be corrupt, all entries in Mii.CharInfoEx (the Mii's design) are empty. The save editor can replace them with the default Mii design if you are trying to recover a save.
panel.fix_zero_charinfoex_done: {count, plural, one {Replaced # Mii's design} other {Replaced # Miis' designs}}.
panel.slot_count: {count, plural, one {# populated slot} other {# populated slots}}

advanced.warning_intro: The advanced panel exposes every raw entry in your save file. Editing values here can permanently corrupt your save, brick your progress, or cause the game to crash or refuse to load. There is no undo.
advanced.warning_backup: Back up your save file first. Copy it somewhere safe before changing anything.
advanced.warning_understand: Only edit entries if you understand what they mean.
advanced.warning_disclaimer: You're on your own. The authors of this tool take no responsibility for lost saves.
advanced.warning_acknowledge: I understand, show the advanced panel
advanced.tree_filter_placeholder: Filter by path, segment, or hash…
advanced.tree_select_prompt: Select an entry from the tree to edit.
advanced.table_intro: Full, searchable list of every entry in the save.
advanced.table_no_match: No entries match the current filters.
advanced.detail_readonly_browsing: {type} browsing is read-only in this editor.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about the editor. Click a question to expand.

brick.intro: Yes, a bad edit can corrupt your save and there's no in-game recovery, so the only way back is the backup you made before editing.
brick.a1: The editor does a few things to prevent corrupting your save:
brick.a2: It never overwrites your original file, it creates a new one for you to download, so if something goes wrong the original is still safe.
brick.a3: The editor will block some edits that are known to cause corruption, and warn you about risky ones.

backup.q: Should I back up my save first?
backup.a: Yes, always. Copy your Mii.sav, Player.sav, and Map.sav files somewhere safe before loading them here. A bad edit can corrupt your save with no in-game recovery, and the only way back is the backup you made.

upload.q: Will my save be uploaded anywhere?
upload.a1: No. The editor runs entirely in your browser, files are parsed and rewritten locally, and your save data isn't sent to any server.
upload.a2_prefix: The site is also open source, so you can read the code yourself on

sav_files.q: What's the difference between Player.sav, Mii.sav, and Map.sav?
sav_files.intro: The game splits its data across three files, and the editor has a tab for each:
sav_files.player: - your player data: your name, island name, money, play time, everything you unlocked...
sav_files.mii: - per-Mii data for everyone living on your island: personalities, voices, relationships...
sav_files.map: - the layout of your island: where each building is placed, what each tile on the ground is...
sav_files.outro: The three files are independent, you can edit one without touching the others, and you only need to upload the ones you actually want to change.

advanced.q: What is the Advanced tab for?
advanced.a1: The structured tabs (Player, Mii, Map) cover the fields the editor knows how to render with proper labels and pickers. The Advanced section, found at the bottom of each tab, is a raw entry browser that lists every key found in the save, including ones that haven't been mapped to a friendly UI yet. Use it if you need to inspect or edit something the structured tabs don't expose.
advanced.a2: Treat it as power-user territory. Some fields are cryptic, and editing them blindly is a good way to corrupt a save, back up first. Anything done here is at your own risk.

hashes.q: Why are some entries shown as numbers or unknown names?
hashes.a: Save files reference content (clothes, food, treasures, room styles, and so on) by hash, not by readable name. The editor ships a hash list that translates as many of those hashes as possible back into names. When an entry shows up as a hex number or as "Unknown", it's a hash that isn't in the list yet. If you identify one, please open an issue or share it on Discord so it can be added for everyone.

stable_beta.q: Stable vs Beta, which should I use?
stable_beta.stable_body: the version you should probably use. This is the tested, polished version of the editor, where features are less likely to be broken and bugs are less likely to cause corruption. Useful for most people, especially if you're new to save editing.
stable_beta.beta_body: the experimental build, where new features land first. Expect more bugs and occasional broken behaviour. Useful if you want to try something that isn't on Stable yet, or if a fix you reported has just been merged.
stable_beta.switch_hint: You can switch between the two using the link next to the version badge in the header. Whichever you pick, always back up your saves first.

translation.q: How do I add a translation?
translation.a_link: Adding a Localization section in the repo's README
translation.a_suffix: , it has the full set of steps.

bug.intro_prefix: Please open an issue on GitHub, the "Report an issue" link in the footer takes you straight to the new-issue form, or you can use
bug.item_what: What you were trying to do.
bug.item_version: Whether you were on Stable or Beta, and the version number from the header.
bug.item_screenshot: A screenshot if it's a UI issue.
bug.item_save: The save file itself if you're comfortable sharing it. Saves help reproduce a bug a lot faster than descriptions alone.
bug.discord_prefix: If you'd rather not file an issue, the

cant_do_x.intro: If something you want to edit isn't in the structured tabs, it's almost always one of two things:
cant_do_x.item_unmapped: Nobody has mapped that field to the editor yet, the data is in the save, but no UI has been built for it.
cant_do_x.item_unsafe: The field can't be edited safely on its own, it might be derived from other values, or part of a structure where changing one piece without the rest would corrupt the save.
cant_do_x.outro: Either way, the right move is to open an issue or ask on Discord describing what you're trying to change. New features get prioritised based on what people actually want.

about.title: About
about.backup_warning_title: Always back up your save files before editing.
about.backup_warning_body: Make a copy of your Mii.sav, Player.sav, and Map.sav files somewhere safe before loading them here. A bad edit can corrupt your save and there is no in-game recovery.
about.intro: A browser-based save editor for Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream (Nintendo Switch). Drop your Mii.sav, Player.sav, and Map.sav files in, edit, and download a patched copy. Everything runs locally - files never leave your machine.

credits.save_structure_note: For the base structure of the save file.
credits.hash_list_note: The hash list shipped to the client.
credits.hash_work_note: For the work on the hash list.
credits.wish_offset_note: For finding the wish counter offset.

about.disclaimer: This is an unofficial, fan-made tool. Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is © Nintendo.
